case.source: Platinum 'Paint: Exterior / Tex-cote / Cool Life' + 'Stucco: New' (Stucco patch) chunks + Exterior Paint / Stucco Repair playbooks
case.description: Exterior repaint plus stucco crack repair — several stucco cracks around the house and peeling paint on the trim. Patch the stucco first, then full exterior repaint. House was built in 1965.

lines: Pull city permit / plan-check as required

Home built in 1965 (pre-1978): test for lead-based paint before any scraping or sanding; if present, follow EPA RRP lead-safe work practices — plastic containment/ground sheeting, wet scraping methods, HEPA vacuum cleanup, certified renovator on site
Cover all working areas: windows, trees, bushes, plants, patio, furniture
Prepare trench, 6" deep all the way around the house
Pressure wash entire exterior to remove chalking, dirt, mildew, and loose material; let dry fully before proceeding
Scrape damaged stucco surface; identify and mark all cracked stucco areas around the house
Open cracks wider and fix cracks; patch walls as needed
Prepare walls: apply scratch and brown coat cement, allow proper cure time
Apply new color coat stucco, texture-matched to existing finish; color and texture selected by customer
Scrape and sand all loose/peeling paint on trim, fascia, eaves, and window/door molding to a sound edge
Sand all wood around the house
Replace damaged wood as needed
Apply wood sealer to sanded and replaced wood
Spot-prime all bare wood, metal, and patched stucco areas with the appropriate primer for the substrate
Caulk all trim joints, window/door perimeters, and siding seams with paintable exterior caulk
Apply primer and 2 coats of paint to body/field color; color selected by customer
Apply primer and 2 coats of paint to trim and accent colors (fascia, eaves, garage door, shutters, front door) after field coat is dry
Final walk: touch up drips, holidays, and overspray; clean overspray off windows, hardscape, and landscaping; remove masking and protection
Cover back trench around the house
Clean up and haul away debris

notes: Client description is isolated stucco crack patching (not full re-stucco), so scope follows the stucco patch sequence, not new/full stucco replacement — confirm with client that cracks are cosmetic/hairline, not structural.

Company's own repaint and patch templates state no permit is typically required for this scope; permit/plan-check line included per standard proposal format — confirm with estimator whether it applies here.
Lead testing assumed necessary given 1965 build date; confirm test results before finalizing prep method and pricing.
Paint tier assumed standard 2-coat acrylic system; confirm with client whether an elastomeric or Cool Coat upgrade is wanted given the active cracking.

groundedOn.title: Exterior Material Mixing, Entry Emphasis, and Landscape Layering
Playbook: Hazmat Gate — Asbestos (AQMD Rule 1403) & Lead (EPA RRP)
